items.title: Teenager arrested after remains found during search for missing girl

children.content: Detectives are investigating after human remains were found during a search for a missing teenage girl in Victoria's west.

gallery.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

items.title: Pies veteran's record run at risk after tackle, as star forward hurt

children.content: Jack Crisp's record run of consectuive games is at risk after a tackle saw his North opponent ruled out with concussion. Follow live.

image.altText: Crisp1

items.title: Regulator closes 45 family day care residences across Melbourne

children.content: For the first time, Victoria's Early Childhood Regulatory Authority has used its powers to suspend a childcare operator, closing 45 family day care residences across Melbourne.

image.altText: Kids toys on the floor.

items.title: Derryn Hinch, 'human headline' and media figure, dies aged 82

children.content: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders, which earned him several stints in jail.

articles.title: Derryn Hinch remembered as 'tough old bugger', 'crusader for justice'

children.content: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

image.altText: A man smiles while posing in front of a long coastline and blue sky.

gallery.altText: Hinch, dressed in a red scarf, talks to reporters outside Parliament House in Canberra

thumbnail.altText: Hinch, dressed in a red scarf, talks to reporters outside Parliament House in Canberra

items.title: Twelve Apostles Visitor Centre worksite shut down after man loses hand

children.content: A workman has lost his hand in a workplace accident while cleaning out a sewer pit along the Great Ocean Road.

image.altText: Tourists stand in gloomy weather staring out to sea at the 12 Apostles.

items.title: Musician loses discrimination fight against orchestra over Gaza comments

children.content: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, 39, bearded, in a suit, including black tie, walks outside a court.

items.title: Two men arrested after Melbourne cafe shooting

children.content: Police arrest two men they allege fired shots into a cafe in Essendon North overnight.

image.altText: The outside of a cafe in a strip of shops with police tape blocking the road.

items.title: Why most train networks dodged the Telstra outage except in Victoria

children.content: Rail services for regional Victorians have resumed after a Telstra outage caused every line to be shut down for a day and a half. Why was the scale of disruption so severe?

image.altText: Two people, one in a purple vest that says "V/Line Customer Service" near a train and a sign saying "not taking passengers".

items.title: Why one of our greatest scientific discoveries is in a UK gift shop

children.content: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

gallery.altText: A meteorite travels through deep space lit up from the inside.

thumbnail.altText: A meteorite travels through deep space lit up from the inside.

items.title: Environmental groups raise concerns about logging 'by stealth'

children.content: In the wake of the Victorian government's move away from native timber harvesting, environmental groups are calling for greater management of timber by-product removals in state forests.

image.altText: A tree stump

items.title: Inside firefighters' high-tech push to prepare for perilous season

children.content: From virtual reality training to drones, these tools are helping firefighters prepare for a fast-approaching, potentially severe bushfire season.

image.altText: A CFA volunteer dressed in firefighting uniform, using a VR hose, pointing it at a TV screen in a brick room

items.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

items.title: Modi tells Melbourne crowd India aspires to 'grow more, achieve more'

children.content: More than 20,000 people have given a rapturous welcome to Narendra Modi at Melbourne's Docklands Stadium as Anthony Albanese heaps praise on Australia's Indian diaspora.

image.altText: Two men, one wearing a gold jacket and the other a suit and tie, hold hands in the air

items.title: As snowfall declines, a key ingredient is keeping the ski season alive

children.content: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

gallery.altText: A group of skiers on the perisher mountainside with ski lifts in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A group of skiers on the perisher mountainside with ski lifts in the background.

items.title: Disability support provider agrees to $2 million back pay deal with workers

children.content: Fair Work Ombudsman Anna Booth says Yooralla's underpayment of staff serves as a warning to employers to comply with minimum payment laws.

image.altText: Image of the Yooralla logo superimposed on a map of Victoria

items.title: Trainee surgeon found with 4,500 videos from hospital bathrooms pleads guilty

children.content: Ryan Cho has pleaded guilty to 13 charges, including stalking, producing an intimate image and installing an optical surveillance device.

image.altText: Ryan Cho wears sunglasses and a surgical mask as he walks down the street. A reporter holds a microphone towards him.

items.title: Melbourne injecting room cut ambulance callouts by 70pc, report reveals

children.content: Data from a new paper shows the number of heroin-related ambulance call-outs dropped from 48 a month to 14 a month in the first five years following the Richmond medically supervised injecting room's opening.

image.altText: A close up of a syringe and needle

items.title: Families concerned over axing of local NDIS-funded program

children.content: An Albury-based NDIS provider says an upcoming change to NDIS funding is one of the reasons it will discontinue a program that builds skills and confidence in those living with a disability.

image.altText: A picture of a building with a sign displaying a sign pof Aspire provider

items.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer will sit next match out after death

children.content: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

gallery.altText: Epping Football Netball Club president Luke Di Vincentis stands near the boundary fence

thumbnail.altText: Epping Football Netball Club president Luke Di Vincentis stands near the boundary fence

items.title: Birds in Melbourne found to have 'highly elevated' PFAS in their blood

children.content: Blood samples found levels of the "forever chemicals" in sparrows near contaminated industrial and military sites were 10 times higher than those in a comparison population.

image.altText: A brown bird sits on a green railing with yellow coloured grassland behind.

items.title: Police investigate two suspicious Melbourne house fires

children.content: The two house fires started within minutes of each other, less than two kilometres apart in Melbourne's north.

image.altText: Police, firefighters and a house occupant.

items.title: Well-connected Melbourne businessman pleads not guilty to raping woman

children.content: The businessman, who cannot be identified for legal reasons, is accused of attacking a woman at his home in 2023.

image.altText: The outside of the County Court of Victoria building.

items.title: Seven arrested as police probe alleged fraud involving regional council

children.content: The men were arrested by detectives as part of an ongoing investigation after a council in Victoria's north says it referred alleged fraud involving council employees to police.

image.altText: A sign that says Greater Shepparton City Council

items.title: The tour that changed how these musicians see Australia

children.content: Half a dozen kids swarm around the drum kit as this Melbourne band rips through a set at full tilt, during a solar-powered tour through some of the NT's most remote communities.

gallery.altText: Tour bus drives along a remote dirt road beside a large termite mound.
Person plays a drum kit as children gather around during a live music performance.
Child carrying an oversized acoustic guitar walks among people beside a touring vehicle in bushland.

thumbnail.altText: Tour bus drives along a remote dirt road beside a large termite mound.
Person plays a drum kit as children gather around during a live music performance.
Child carrying an oversized acoustic guitar walks among people beside a touring vehicle in bushland.

items.title: Brothers accused of killing stepfather outside primary school face court

children.content: DNA testing in the investigation of two brothers accused of murdering their stepfather outside a Melbourne primary school could take up to three months, a court hears.

image.altText: A Google street view screen grab shows a sign reading 'Strathaird Primary School'.

items.title: CFA leaders attempt to recall 'draft' submission to fatal summer fire inquiry

children.content: An administrative error during a parliamentary inquiry has led the CFA to inadvertently reveal exactly how short-staffed its stations were during last summer's deadly fire season.

image.altText: A CFA truck with a handpainted sign reading "Go Fight Your Own Fires Jacinta Allan"

items.title: Toddler waits seven hours for stitches during patient surge

children.content: A Central Victorian mother says she waited seven hours at Bendigo Health for her toddler to be treated for a head wound.

image.altText: A little girl sitting in an ambulance bed with a head wound.

items.title: Embattled MP Moira Deeming apologises to supporters in social media video

children.content: The Liberal MP thanked her supporters and apologised for "distractions" in a video posted to her Facebook page, as questions still loom over her political future.

image.altText: Moira Deeming

items.title: Winmar statue removed during NAIDOC Week after assault conviction

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

gallery.altText: Bronze statue of Nicky Winmar at Perth Stadium.

thumbnail.altText: Bronze statue of Nicky Winmar at Perth Stadium.

items.title: With women's game short on resources this AFLW player went to Doha for ACL rehab

children.content: Essendon AFLW forward Emily Gough said devastating ACL injuries can get lost in the sheer amount of them in women's game.

image.altText: Essendon AFLW player went to Doha for specialised ACL rehabilitation.

items.title: Alleged Pam the Bird graffiti vandal faces court after bridge stand-off

children.content: A man has faced court after spending hours at the top of a 140-metre-high tower on Melbourne's Bolte Bridge, where he allegedly spray-painted the notorious graffiti symbol.

image.altText: A man dressed in a black top and pants walks along the rocks protruding from a river with his hands in the air.
